pages.raw_text:
The last wreck Friday morning
near Hillyard, in which two men
were killed and another terribly
injured, has afforded Coroner
Witter a splendid opportunity to
demonstrate his fidelity to the
railroads.


Up to last night no official In
quiry had been made according to
the coroner's admissions. He is
quoted then as stating that he
would take the matter up with the
claim agent of the Great Northern
today.


The railroad claim agent is an
official retained expressly to defend
the company from all responsibility
possible for accidents.


With the claim agent the cor
oner of this county will talk over
the fatal wreck. Presumably what
ever the claim agent thinks about it
will be the coroner's official find
ing.


Probably no greater neglect of
duty, either through intention or
incompetency, was ever perpetrated
on community than this action OF
Writer'S.


Two men were killed and third
was expected to die. The Press
had little difficulty in getting a
statement from Howard Leuton
the injured engineer. Coroner
Witter had not tried to do so UP
to last night.


Ever since the wreck it had been
expected that Leuton would die. He
is the only one who had the evil
dence that the company was to
blame for the wreck in sending
him out on mistaken orders.


Is 1t possible that the county
coroner is waiting for him to die
before beginning an investigation
to fix the blame?


Today the coroner refused to
talk about the matter.


Leuton is reported slightly i'm
proved today.
